refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability ≥ 0.999 (99.9%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the bidirectional command-and-telemetry link is operational to total evaluation time.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-010 addresses a critical operational requirement: remote operators must reliably command and receive feedback from equipment. The 0.999 threshold (approximately 8.76 hours downtime per year) is a standard for safety-critical teleoperation systems. Refinement clarifies measurement scope (bidirectional link), evaluation period (customer-defined), and the quantitative target.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous monitoring of link state transitions (up/down) during the evaluation period; automated logging of outage duration and root cause; statistical analysis of availability = (total_time - total_downtime) / total_time.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall establish and maintain a bidirectional command link from tele-operator to vehicle.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall establish and maintain a bidirectional telemetry link from vehicle to tele-operator.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall detect link degradation or loss and trigger failsafe actions within 100 ms.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall log all link state changes and availability metrics for post-evaluation analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Redundant network paths (primary + backup) with automatic failover; dual-modem architecture; geographic diversity of base stations.
DP-B: Single high-reliability network provider with contractual SLA ≥ 0.999; no redundancy; reliance on provider infrastructure.
DP-C: Hybrid approach: primary commercial network + secondary satellite/mesh backup; intelligent link selection based on signal quality metrics.
DP-D: Dedicated private network (licensed spectrum) with owned base stations; eliminates dependency on third-party providers.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid approach with primary commercial network (LTE/5G) and secondary satellite or mesh backup; intelligent link selection based on real-time signal quality (RSRP, latency, packet loss); automatic switchover on primary link degradation; continuous availability monitoring and logging.

potential_sources: DP-C couples teleoperation link availability to network infrastructure selection (Teleoperation module) and safety failsafe logic (Safety Services module); both are within scope and manageable.
Signal quality metrics (RSRP, latency) couple to vehicle telemetry collection; this is a natural dependency, not a design flaw.
Switchover logic couples to operator training and procedures; mitigation via clear HMI feedback and failsafe defaults.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C avoids over-coupling to a single provider (DP-B risk) and avoids capital-intensive private network (DP-D cost). Hybrid redundancy is industry-standard for safety-critical teleoperation.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Secondary backup network (satellite or mesh) is available and contractually secured at the worksite.
Risk: Satellite latency (500+ ms) may exceed operator tolerance; mitigation: use low-latency mesh or negotiate latency SLA.
Risk: Switchover logic may introduce transient command loss; mitigation: buffer commands during switchover and replay on link recovery.
Risk: Operator may not notice link switch; mitigation: prominent HMI indicator and audible alert.
Assumption: Evaluation period is ≥ 1 year to achieve statistical significance for 0.999 target.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to specify: (1) primary network technology (LTE Cat-M1 or 5G NR); (2) secondary backup technology (LEO satellite or LoRa mesh); (3) switchover latency budget (< 500 ms); (4) signal quality thresholds for triggering switchover; (5) logging granularity (per-second state snapshots).

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Owns the end-to-end teleoperation link stack: network interface selection, modem management, link state machine, failover logic, signal quality monitoring, and availability logging. Includes both primary and backup network drivers and the intelligent link selection algorithm.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands (movement, tool control, mode changes) from tele-operator console; Teleoperation module buffers and queues commands during link switchover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits vehicle state (position, orientation, sensor readings, link quality metrics) to tele-operator console; Teleoperation module prioritizes telemetry during bandwidth constraints.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints from Safety Services (e.g., geofence, speed limits); sends link availability status to Safety Services for failsafe decision-making.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Abstracts primary and backup network interfaces; Teleoperation module manages modem drivers, signal quality polling, and switchover triggers.

module_containment_scores.notes: Teleoperation is the natural owner of link availability logic. Minimal diffusion: only SafetyAlert coupling (failsafe trigger) and Tasking/Telemetry flow-through. High cohesion within module.

Safety Services owns failsafe policy (e.g., stop vehicle on link loss) but NOT link availability measurement or switchover logic. Assigning DP-C to Safety Services would scatter network management across two modules.
Vehicle owns physical modem hardware but not link management logic. Assigning DP-C to Vehicle would couple vehicle firmware to network provider specifics, reducing modularity.
NGHE Solution is the system-level container but not the functional owner of teleoperation link management. Assigning DP-C here would bypass architectural layering.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the single, cohesive owner of DP-C (hybrid network redundancy and intelligent link selection). It encapsulates all link state management, failover logic, and availability monitoring. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and minimal.
containment_risks: Risk: Modem driver updates (primary/backup) may require Vehicle module coordination; mitigation: define clear driver API boundary in Network Link interface.
Risk: Safety failsafe logic (e.g., stop on link loss) may be implemented in both Teleoperation and Safety Services; mitigation: Safety Services owns policy, Teleoperation owns detection and alert.
Risk: Operator training and HMI feedback for link switchover may be scattered across multiple teams; mitigation: Teleoperation module owns HMI contract (SafetyAlert interface includes link status).
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the Network Link interface specification: (1) define modem driver API (init, connect, disconnect, signal_quality_poll); (2) specify failover trigger thresholds (RSRP < -120 dBm, latency > 500 ms); (3) document logging schema (timestamp, link_state, signal_quality, switchover_reason).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid redundancy with intelligent link selection) directly addresses FR-NGHE-010 (achieve 0.999 link availability). The DP is specific, measurable, and feasible. Hybrid approach balances cost, complexity, and reliability. No design-functional mismatch detected.

diffusion_risks: Modem driver management: Teleoperation module depends on Vehicle module for hardware abstraction; mitigated by clear Network Link interface.
Failsafe policy: Safety Services module defines stop-on-link-loss policy; Teleoperation module detects and alerts; clear separation of concerns.
Operator HMI: Teleoperation module owns link status display; Safety Services owns safety-critical alerts; minimal overlap.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module achieves 0.95 containment score. All link management logic (redundancy, switchover, monitoring) is cohesively owned. External dependencies (Network Link, SafetyAlert) are well-scoped and necessary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Availability measurement is straightforward: log link state transitions and compute uptime ratio. Evaluation period (customer-defined, typically 1 year) is feasible. Automated monitoring tools (e.g., syslog, SNMP) can collect data.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-010 is allocated to Teleoperation module. Sub-FRs (010.1 command link, 010.2 telemetry link, 010.3 failsafe detection, 010.4 logging) are all within Teleoperation scope. Clear traceability from FR to DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: Refine Network Link interface specification with modem driver API and failover thresholds.
Define SafetyAlert interface contract: what link status information does Teleoperation send to Safety Services?
Specify logging schema and retention policy for availability audit.
Document operator training requirements for link switchover scenarios.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-010 is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs (command link, telemetry link, failsafe detection, logging). Each sub-FR requires further design detail. DP-C is sound but needs refinement (network technologies, switchover thresholds, logging schema). Teleoperation module assignment is correct and well-contained. Proceed to next decomposition level to detail sub-FRs and DP specifics.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall establish and maintain a bidirectional command link from tele-operator to vehicle with latency ≤ 200 ms (99th percentile).
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall establish and maintain a bidirectional telemetry link from vehicle to tele-operator with update rate ≥ 10 Hz and latency ≤ 500 ms.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall detect link degradation (RSRP < -120 dBm or latency > 500 ms) and trigger automatic failover to backup network within 100 ms.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall log all link state changes (timestamp, link_state, signal_quality, switchover_reason) with ≥ 1 Hz granularity for post-evaluation analysis.

revise.why: DP-C requires refinement: specify primary network technology (LTE Cat-M1 or 5G NR), secondary backup (LEO satellite or LoRa mesh), switchover latency budget, signal quality thresholds, and logging schema. Current DP is directionally correct but lacks implementation detail.

assumptions: Secondary backup network (satellite or mesh) is available and contractually secured at the worksite.
Evaluation period is ≥ 1 year to achieve statistical significance for 0.999 target.
Operator tolerance for link switchover latency is ≤ 500 ms (based on typical teleoperation HMI responsiveness).
Modem hardware (primary + backup) is available and integrated into Vehicle module.
Safety Services module owns failsafe policy (stop vehicle on link loss); Teleoperation module owns detection and alert.
open_risks: Satellite latency (500+ ms) may exceed operator tolerance; requires latency SLA negotiation or mesh alternative.
Switchover logic may introduce transient command loss; requires command buffering and replay mechanism.
Operator may not notice link switch; requires prominent HMI indicator and audible alert.
Modem driver updates may require Vehicle module coordination; requires clear driver API boundary.
Logging overhead may impact real-time performance; requires performance testing and buffer sizing.
evidence_needed: Network provider SLA documentation (primary LTE/5G and secondary satellite/mesh).
Latency and packet loss measurements from candidate backup networks.
Operator feedback on acceptable switchover latency and HMI feedback.
Modem driver API specification and integration plan with Vehicle module.
Logging performance analysis (CPU, memory, storage impact).
Failsafe policy document from Safety Services (stop-on-link-loss criteria).
